reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing immutable conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: Generation runs off the main thread in models/gpt2-text-generation/worker.js. Rather than the pipeline's generate(), it runs its OWN autoregressive decode loop over AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained('Xenova/gpt2', { dtype:'int8', device:'wasm' }): each step does model(makeInputs(ids)) → last-position logits → top-k → softmax sampling (or greedy), feeds the chosen token back, and reports the chosen token's probability + the top-8 candidates. Conformance calls-real-inference (source) and loads-clean tested pass; io-shape-semantic is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: Every number (chosen prob, top-k candidates) is the model's real output over the 50,257-token BPE vocab — that is what powers the see-inside 'watch the model pick' surface and the choose-the-next-token game. int8 on WASM runs everywhere; no WebGPU gate. No fresh live run performed in this backfill, so no measured latency is claimed.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = an autoregressive continuation of the raw prompt (GPT-2 has no chat template). The worker streams { piece, generated, chosen:{token,prob}, topk:[{token,prob,chosen}] } per step and a final { text, generated, ms, tokens }; the distribution() op returns top-k next-token candidates for the interactive branching demo.
rubric.notes: maxNew is capped (≤80) and EOS (50256) breaks the loop; sampling uses temperature+top-k, greedy is offered.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/gpt2, linked), task = text-generation, backend = WASM, dtype = int8, size ≈280 MB, license MIT. Conformance declares-model-id/declares-task/declares-quantisation (int8)/declares-backend tested pass. env.allowLocalModels=false lets the library own its Cache Storage.
rubric.notes: The worker MODEL_ID matches the declared id; no invented API surface.

rubric.evidence: Loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (conformance shared-auto-init-loader source pass); WASM/int8 has no WebGPU requirement, so it auto-initialises on any device with no Load click.
rubric.notes: auto-init-on-cached is manual-evidenced; progress_callback drives the loader.

rubric.evidence: Loading/progress/ready/error announced in role=status aria-live (live-status-region pass); worker errors surface on the page (errors-on-the-page source pass). Runs on WASM everywhere, so there is no needs-WebGPU dead-end.
rubric.notes: webgpu-capability-honest is not required for this demo (backend is WASM).

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: the prompt textarea, greedy/sample mode buttons (mGreedy/mSample), max-new/temperature/top-k range inputs, Run and Stop. The interactive branching demo lets you pick from the real top-k next tokens.
No canned output — the continuation and every probability come from the model's real logits (own decode loop). no-fake-output is a manual-evidenced assertion.

Conformance buttons-have-names + images-have-alt (script) and live-status-region (dom, [role=status][aria-live]) tested pass at build. The status line is role=status aria-live=polite; the chat log is aria-live. HOWEVER the responsive pre-scan flagged sub-24px tap targets pending agent verification (support=needs-review, automatedNotes: 9 on desktop / 6 on mobile — mostly the range-slider thumbs and small secondary buttons), below this repo's ~44px tap-target bar.
rubric.notes: Light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system (public/styles.css). The sub-24px flag is the real open a11y item on this demo.

rubric.evidence: Layout uses fluid flex/grid: controls flex-wrap, the chat/transcript log is intrinsically sized (min/max-block-size + overflow-y), textareas/canvas use inline-size, and the see-inside stat grid is auto-fit minmax. Conformance responsive-desktop + responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) are in the immutable suite. support=needs-review on both classes: the automated pre-scan reported 9 sub-24px target(s) desktop / 6 mobile awaiting an agent matrix pass — no live matrix was run in this backfill. Runs on WASM/int8 on any device (no WebGPU gate); ≈280 MB download.
rubric.notes: I did NOT run responsive-check in this retrospective pass, so I do not claim a measured 0px-overflow result; the honest status is needs-review with the sub-24px tap-target flag.

rubric.evidence: The decode loop + vocab-wide softmax run in the Web Worker, off the UI thread (inference-off-main-thread source pass); the main thread only paints streamed pieces and top-k bars. int8-on-WASM decoding is inherently slower than WebGPU, but it never blocks the interface.
rubric.notes: topkIndices uses a bounded partial selection (not a full 50k sort) each step. gpt2 has no CSS animation, so no prefers-reduced-motion guard is needed.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low off main thread web worker streaming tokens
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep interactions responsive (INP): move heavy work off the main thread; avoid long blocking tasks.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the autoregressive decode loop and vocab softmax run entirely in a dedicated module Worker; the UI thread only paints streamed pieces and probability bars.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js own decode loop off-thread; conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: break up long tasks main thread responsiveness
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break up heavy synchronous processing so the browser can handle input and repaint.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — generation is a per-step streamed loop in the worker posting one message per token, not a single blocking main-thread call; top-k selection is a bounded partial-select, not a full 50k sort.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: generate() posts per-step; topkIndices bounded selection in worker.js

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates aria-live
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; keyboard operability.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live announcements + accessible button names (buttons-have-names pass). EXCEPTION pending: sub-24px tap targets flagged for an agent matrix pass.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance live-status-region + buttons-have-names pass; support=needs-review sub-24px note

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — controls flex-wrap and the top-k rows use a grid; no fixed widths forcing horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-desktop/mobile assertions in suite; flex-wrap controls

openQuestions: Verify and enlarge the 9 desktop / 6 mobile sub-24px tap targets (range-slider thumbs / mode buttons) to ~44px, then flip support to ok with a real matrix pass.
int8-on-WASM decode is slow on low-end mobile — worth surfacing an explicit expectation and/or a smaller maxNew default there.

followUpGoals.goal: Run scripts/responsive-check.mjs at 360×740 and 1280×800, enlarge the flagged sub-24px tap targets, record support=ok per class.
Consider an optional WebGPU path (q4f16) for GPT-2 to speed up the decode game on capable devices while keeping the WASM default.

summary: Revision-1 retrospective critique backfill for gpt2-text-generation (Xenova/gpt2, text-generation, Transformers.js, WASM/int8, ≈280 MB, MIT). Evidence grounded in code inspection + the immutable conformance suite (calls-real-inference, shared-auto-init-loader, inference-off-main-thread, declares-*, live-status-region, buttons-have-names tested pass). Distinctive demo: an OWN autoregressive decode loop over AutoModelForCausalLM that exposes the real full next-token distribution each step (chosen prob + top-8 candidates) — powering the see-inside 'watch the model pick' and the choose-the-next-token game. Runs on WASM everywhere (no WebGPU gate). GENUINE OPEN ITEMS: support=needs-review with 9/6 sub-24px tap targets pending a matrix pass, and WASM/int8 decode is inherently slow on low-end mobile. No fresh live-inference numbers claimed.
